description: §6.6.9 / §19.9.9 (RULING: THE SPLIT) — a CPS-split fn (`@lastRows = ?{…}`) reading a `const <total>` DERIVED marshals it (client stub sends `{ "total": _scrml_derived_get("total") }`, server reads `_scrml_body["total"]`) AND fires W-SERVER-DERIVED-MARSHAL (severity warning): a client-computed derived aggregate is a client-supplied snapshot worth a trust nudge, even though it now works. NOT an error (the value crosses the wire). Asserts the warning fires and E-REACTIVE-003 stays absent.
rationale: §6.6.9: a CPS fn marshals a derived read AND warns (W-SERVER-DERIVED-MARSHAL) — the round-trip works but the value is a client-computed snapshot, not server-recomputed.
